title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU-first fidelity route: scan over a larger pannable painterly Roman terrain map, hover oversized highlighted regions, click to trigger a cinematic dive, and reveal a primitive-built full-bodied battlefield tableau.

fidelityFocus.terrain: painterly low-poly terrain with warm glaze colors, brush-stroke overlays, river ribbon, contour accents, and larger extents
fidelityFocus.mapNavigation: large pannable region-selection surface with keyboard/drag pan and wheel zoom
fidelityFocus.regions: oversized highlighted region meshes with glow, wobble borders, and DSK affordance descriptors
fidelityFocus.soldiers: full-bodied primitive soldiers built from boxes/quads/triangles: legs, boots, torso, cuirass, arms, head, helmet, crest, shield, spear, cape, shadow
fidelityFocus.battlefield: primitive formations, banners, field strokes, trampled center lane, atmospheric fog

protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.
currentLocalSurfaces: painterly terrain mesh color and brush-stroke overlay generation
large pannable region ellipse placement and labels
cinematic camera interpolation and map pan/zoom controls

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: procedural terrain mesh and terrain color logic
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable visual terrain foundation for campaign-map routes.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable visual-material layer for painterly map surfaces.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable replacement for point-node strategy maps.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.source: keyboard/drag pan, wheel zoom, and map-centered camera focus
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable inspection control for large campaign-map surfaces.

formation layout, banner placement, shadow placement, and battlefield tableau staging
futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable army-tableau visualization without combat simulation.

nextLedge: Add a browser-backed route smoke for the live endpoint that pans the map, selects a highlighted region, reaches the battlefield tableau, and validates DSK-backed fidelity metadata.
